license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7fghjjw
title: Tranformation of Thalassiosira pseudonana via bacterial conjugation
author: Ana Cristina Jaramillo Madrid

steps_raw.instruction: Inoculate 5 mL LB medium (gentamicin+antibiotic 2) with bacterial colonies from the gentamicin +antibiotic 2 plates. Grow overnight.
Start a 150 mL LB subculture with the 5 mL overnight culture (recommended starting OD 600 either 0.05 or 0.1).
Grow at 37 0 C until OD600 reaches 0.4. (A range of OD 600 from 0.4 to 0.6 has worked for me)
Centrifuge at 4000 g, 10 0 C, for 10 min.
Decant supernatant and resuspend in 800 µL SOC.

Measure the Thalassiosira pseudonana cell concentration and calculate the required volume needed to collect 2 x 10 8 cells. Tp cells are cultured in L1 medium. Note: We do not know if cell density before spinning cells down matters. We have successfully tried spinning cells down at ~2-4 x 10 6 cells/mL
Mix 200 µL diatom cells and 200 µL E. coli cells in a 1.5 mL tube.
Pipette up and down a few times.
Plate on 1/2xL1 1% agar plates w/ 5% LB. Note : Make sure the plates are dry.
Incubate in dark at 30 0 C for 90 minutes.
Move plates to standard diatom growth conditions. Incubate 20-24 hrs Note: Supposed to be 18 0 C and constant light, but we just leave them at RT constant light.
Add 1 mL L1 medium and scrape.
Plate 200 µL of the resulting suspension on pre-dried 1/2xL1 1% agar plates w/ 50 ug/ml nourseothricin.
Leave at 18oC and constant light until colonies appear in ~10-12 days.
Decant supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 mL L1 medium. Final concentration 2 x 108 cells/m
